categories.title: Planning Tips
tips: Buying Disney gift cards before your trip is a good way to save spending money for the trip!
Take advantage of the ability to make Lightning Lane reservations before your trip.
Make sure to download the My Disney Experience app on your phone before you leave for Disney.
Try and look at maps before your trip — studying the park layouts can help you feel confident when you arrive.
Unless you are going right in the middle of summer, bring at least one sweater. It can get cold in the Florida winter.
Bring pennies and quarters for pressed penny machines — a great cheap souvenir.
You can have things shipped to your resort room. A common choice is a pack of bottled water.
Make a wishlist of rides everyone wants to experience.
If you are traveling with kids, have them make a list of characters they want to meet.
Bring comfy shoes!

categories.title: Activity Tips
tips: Don't forget to leave some time to relax. Give yourself time to explore your resort and take a dip in the pool.
Give yourself enough time to explore Disney Springs and the Boardwalk area.
The Boardwalk has nightly entertainment — jugglers, magicians, and fun boardwalk-style games.
Disney World has two miniature golf options: Fantasia Gardens and Winter Summerland.
There are two waterparks on property: Typhoon Lagoon and Blizzard Beach.
Stop by Ghirardelli at Disney Springs — you might get a free piece of chocolate.
Take in a show during your next trip! Check out entertainment options at the parks and resorts.

categories.title: Resort Tips
tips: Take a day to explore the Disney resort hotels — the themed details are incredible!
Don't forget about all the dining experiences at the resort hotels.
If you are staying at one of the Epcot Resorts there is a 'back door' entrance via the International Gateway.
Many of the resorts have programs that will watch your children if you'd like a date night.
Club level rooms give you access to a lounge with food and beverages throughout the day.
Get familiar with your resort layout before you travel — some are quite large.
Many of the Deluxe Resort villas have full kitchens and washers and dryers.

categories.title: Park Tips
tips: Arrive at the parks at rope drop for shorter wait times on popular attractions.
Take a midday break at your resort — the parks are less crowded in the early morning and evening.
Use the My Disney Experience app to monitor wait times in real time.
Many restaurants at Disney World require reservations — book as early as possible.
Refillable resort mugs can save you money on drinks throughout your stay.
Check park hours and special event schedules before your trip.
The Main Street Electrical Parade and fireworks are must-sees at Magic Kingdom.

categories.title: Dining Tips
tips: Dining reservations can be made 60 days in advance — set a reminder!
Character dining is a great way to meet multiple characters without waiting in line.
Be sure to mention any food allergies when making reservations and again with your server.
Disney restaurants are very accommodating for dietary restrictions.
Splitting entrees is common and accepted — portions can be very large.
Quick service dining is a great option for busy park days.
The food courts at Value Resorts offer surprisingly good options at reasonable prices.

categories.title: Transportation Tips
tips: Disney's complimentary transportation includes buses, monorail, Skyliner, and boats.
The Skyliner gondola system connects Epcot-area resorts to Hollywood Studios and Epcot.
The monorail runs between Magic Kingdom, the Transportation and Ticket Center, and monorail resorts.
Factor in extra transportation time, especially during park opening and closing.
Uber and Lyft are allowed on Disney property if you prefer to travel independently.
Magical Express (now third-party Mears Connect) offers airport-to-resort transportation.
